name: Breast Cancer Wisconsin
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_breast_cancer
import pandas as pd
data = load_breast_cancer()
df = pd.DataFrame(data.data, columns=data.feature_names)
df['target'] = data.target
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 569 samples of breast cancer tumor measurements. 30 numerical features computed from digitized images of fine needle aspirate. Binary classification: malignant vs benign.
features_description: 30 features: mean, standard error, worst values of radius, texture, perimeter, area, smoothness, compactness, concavity, symmetry

name: Wine Quality Recognition
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_wine
import pandas as pd
data = load_wine()
df = pd.DataFrame(data.data, columns=data.feature_names)
df['target'] = data.target
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 178 wine samples from 3 cultivars in Italy. 13 chemical analysis features. Multiclass classification problem.
features_description: 13 features: alcohol, malic acid, ash, alkalinity, magnesium, phenols, flavanoids, nonflavanoid phenols, proanthocyanins, color intensity, hue, OD280/OD315, proline

name: Handwritten Digits
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_digits
import pandas as pd
data = load_digits()
df = pd.DataFrame(data.data)
df['target'] = data.target
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 1797 grayscale 8x8 images of handwritten digits 0-9. 64 pixel features. Classic multiclass classification for digit recognition.
features_description: 64 features: pixel values (0-16) of 8x8 image flattened into 1D array

name: Iris Flower Classification
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris
import pandas as pd
data = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(data.data, columns=data.feature_names)
df['target'] = data.target
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 150 iris flower samples from 3 species. 4 morphological features. Classic multiclass classification benchmark dataset.
features_description: 4 features: sepal length, sepal width, petal length, petal width (all in cm)

name: Diabetes Progression
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import load_diabetes
import pandas as pd
data = load_diabetes()
df = pd.DataFrame(data.data, columns=data.feature_names)
df['target'] = data.target
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 442 diabetes patients with 10 baseline features. Target is quantitative measure of disease progression after one year. Regression problem.
features_description: 10 features: age, sex, bmi, blood pressure, s1-s6 serum measurements (all normalized)

name: California Housing Prices
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import fetch_california_housing
import pandas as pd
data = fetch_california_housing()
df = pd.DataFrame(data.data, columns=data.feature_names)
df['target'] = data.target
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 20640 California census tract housing data. 8 features about demographics and geography. Regression to predict median house value.
features_description: 8 features: median income, house age, average rooms, average bedrooms, population, average occupancy, latitude, longitude

name: Synthetic Classification Dataset
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import make_classification
import pandas as pd
X, y = make_classification(n_samples=1000, n_features=15, n_informative=10, n_redundant=3, random_state=42)
feature_names = [f'feature_{i}' for i in range(X.shape[1])]
df = pd.DataFrame(X, columns=feature_names)
df['target'] = y
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: Configurable synthetic binary or multiclass classification dataset. Useful for fraud detection, churn prediction, risk scoring, and any custom classification scenario.
features_description: Configurable features: n_informative informative, n_redundant redundant, n_repeated repeated features

name: Synthetic Regression Dataset
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import make_regression
import pandas as pd
X, y = make_regression(n_samples=1000, n_features=15, n_informative=10, noise=0.1, random_state=42)
feature_names = [f'feature_{i}' for i in range(X.shape[1])]
df = pd.DataFrame(X, columns=feature_names)
df['target'] = y
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: Configurable synthetic regression dataset. Useful for sales prediction, price estimation, demand forecasting, and any custom regression scenario.
features_description: Configurable features with specified informative features and Gaussian noise

name: Synthetic Clustering Dataset
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import make_blobs
import pandas as pd
X, y = make_blobs(n_samples=1000, centers=4, n_features=10, random_state=42)
feature_names = [f'feature_{i}' for i in range(X.shape[1])]
df = pd.DataFrame(X, columns=feature_names)
df['cluster'] = y
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: Synthetic isotropic Gaussian blobs for clustering. Useful for customer segmentation, market segmentation, anomaly detection, and unsupervised learning tasks.
features_description: Configurable features generated from Gaussian distributions centered at specified cluster centers

name: Synthetic Non-Linear Classification (Moons)
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import make_moons
import pandas as pd
X, y = make_moons(n_samples=1000, noise=0.1, random_state=42)
df = pd.DataFrame(X, columns=['feature_1', 'feature_2'])
df['target'] = y
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: Two interleaving half-circle clusters. Tests non-linear classifiers. Useful for demonstrating SVM, neural networks, and ensemble methods.
features_description: 2 features representing 2D coordinates of moon-shaped clusters

name: Synthetic Non-Linear Classification (Circles)
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import make_circles
import pandas as pd
X, y = make_circles(n_samples=1000, noise=0.05, factor=0.5, random_state=42)
df = pd.DataFrame(X, columns=['feature_1', 'feature_2'])
df['target'] = y
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: Large circle containing a small circle. Tests kernel methods and non-linear classifiers. Useful for demonstrating RBF-SVM and deep learning.
features_description: 2 features representing 2D coordinates of concentric circle clusters

name: 20 Newsgroups Text
load_code:
from sklearn.datasets import fetch_20newsgroups
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
import pandas as pd
data = fetch_20newsgroups(subset='train', remove=('headers', 'footers', 'quotes'))
df = pd.DataFrame({'text': data.data, 'target': data.target})
df['category'] = df['target'].map(dict(enumerate(data.target_names)))
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 18846 newsgroup posts from 20 different categories. Classic NLP text classification dataset. Topics include politics, religion, sports, technology.
features_description: Raw text posts from 20 newsgroup categories

name: MNIST Handwritten Digits
load_code:
from tensorflow.keras.datasets import mnist
import numpy as np
(X_train, y_train), (X_test, y_test) = mnist.load_data()
X_train = X_train.reshape(-1, 28*28) / 255.0
X_test = X_test.reshape(-1, 28*28) / 255.0
print(f'Train: {X_train.shape}, Test: {X_test.shape}')
description: 70000 grayscale 28x28 images of handwritten digits 0-9. 60k train, 10k test. Benchmark deep learning dataset for image classification.
features_description: 784 features (28x28 pixels, values 0-255) per image

name: Fashion MNIST
load_code:
from tensorflow.keras.datasets import fashion_mnist
import numpy as np
(X_train, y_train), (X_test, y_test) = fashion_mnist.load_data()
X_train = X_train.reshape(-1, 28*28) / 255.0
X_test = X_test.reshape(-1, 28*28) / 255.0
class_names = ['T-shirt', 'Trouser', 'Pullover', 'Dress', 'Coat', 'Sandal', 'Shirt', 'Sneaker', 'Bag', 'Boot']
print(f'Train: {X_train.shape}, Test: {X_test.shape}')
description: 70000 grayscale 28x28 images of fashion items in 10 categories. Drop-in replacement for MNIST. More challenging than digit recognition.
features_description: 784 features (28x28 pixels) per image, 10 clothing categories

name: CIFAR-10 Object Recognition
load_code:
from tensorflow.keras.datasets import cifar10
import numpy as np
(X_train, y_train), (X_test, y_test) = cifar10.load_data()
X_train = X_train.astype('float32') / 255.0
X_test = X_test.astype('float32') / 255.0
class_names = ['airplane','automobile','bird','cat','deer','dog','frog','horse','ship','truck']
print(f'Train: {X_train.shape}, Test: {X_test.shape}')
description: 60000 color 32x32 images in 10 object classes. 50k train, 10k test. Benchmark CNN dataset requiring deeper architectures than MNIST.
features_description: 3072 features (32x32x3 RGB pixels) per image, 10 object classes

name: CIFAR-100 Fine-Grained Recognition
load_code:
from tensorflow.keras.datasets import cifar100
import numpy as np
(X_train, y_train), (X_test, y_test) = cifar100.load_data()
X_train = X_train.astype('float32') / 255.0
X_test = X_test.astype('float32') / 255.0
print(f'Train: {X_train.shape}, Test: {X_test.shape}')
description: 60000 color 32x32 images in 100 fine-grained classes organized in 20 superclasses. More challenging than CIFAR-10 requiring transfer learning.
features_description: 3072 features (32x32x3 RGB pixels) per image, 100 fine-grained classes

name: IMDB Movie Reviews Sentiment
load_code:
from tensorflow.keras.datasets import imdb
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.sequence import pad_sequences
(X_train, y_train), (X_test, y_test) = imdb.load_data(num_words=10000)
X_train = pad_sequences(X_train, maxlen=200)
X_test = pad_sequences(X_test, maxlen=200)
print(f'Train: {X_train.shape}, Test: {X_test.shape}')
description: 50000 IMDB movie reviews labeled as positive or negative. 25k train, 25k test. Classic NLP sentiment analysis dataset for RNN/LSTM/transformer models.
features_description: Integer sequences (word indices from vocabulary of 88k words)

name: Reuters News Topics
load_code:
from tensorflow.keras.datasets import reuters
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.sequence import pad_sequences
(X_train, y_train), (X_test, y_test) = reuters.load_data(num_words=10000)
X_train = pad_sequences(X_train, maxlen=200)
X_test = pad_sequences(X_test, maxlen=200)
print(f'Train: {X_train.shape}, Test: {X_test.shape}')
description: 11228 Reuters newswire articles categorized into 46 topics. Multiclass NLP classification dataset for topic modeling and text classification.
features_description: Integer sequences (word indices) from Reuters newswires

name: Movie Reviews Sentiment (NLTK)
load_code:
import nltk
nltk.download('movie_reviews')
from nltk.corpus import movie_reviews
import pandas as pd
docs = [(list(movie_reviews.words(fileid)), category)
        for category in movie_reviews.categories()
        for fileid in movie_reviews.fileids(category)]
df = pd.DataFrame(docs, columns=['words', 'sentiment'])
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 2000 movie reviews labeled as positive or negative. Classic NLP dataset from Cornell. Used for text classification and sentiment analysis with traditional ML methods.
features_description: Raw tokenized text reviews, positive and negative categories

name: Reuters Corpus (NLTK)
load_code:
import nltk
nltk.download('reuters')
from nltk.corpus import reuters
import pandas as pd
docs = [(reuters.raw(fileid), reuters.categories(fileid)[0])
        for fileid in reuters.fileids()
        if len(reuters.categories(fileid)) == 1]
df = pd.DataFrame(docs, columns=['text', 'category'])
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
description: 10788 Reuters news documents from 1987 in 90 categories. Used for multi-label text classification and information retrieval research.
features_description: Raw text news articles with single or multiple category labels

name: Emotion Text Classification
load_code:
from datasets import load_dataset
import pandas as pd
ds = load_dataset("dair-ai/emotion", "split")
df_train = ds["train"].to_pandas()
df_test = ds["test"].to_pandas()
df = pd.concat([df_train, df_test], ignore_index=True)
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
print(df["label"].value_counts())
description: 20000 English Twitter messages labeled with 6 emotions: sadness, joy, love, anger, fear, surprise. Multiclass text classification for emotion detection.
features_description: Features: text (tweet), label (emotion category 0-5)

name: AG News Topic Classification
load_code:
from datasets import load_dataset
import pandas as pd
ds = load_dataset("ag_news")
df_train = ds["train"].to_pandas()
df_test = ds["test"].to_pandas()
df = pd.concat([df_train, df_test], ignore_index=True)
print(df.shape)
print(df.head())
print(df["label"].value_counts())
description: 120000 news articles from 4 categories: World, Sports, Business, Sci/Tech. Used for topic classification, text representation, and transformer fine-tuning benchmarks.
features_description: Features: text (news article), label (topic category 0-3)
